zh.lifeSummary: Baron Eudes De Rye是Hubert De Ryes Steward Of Normandy和Albreda Preaux的兒子，出生在法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯也斯。他和Rohese FitzRichard de Clare至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1079年在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國艾可可爾斯特聖 越恩斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Rye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone living at a place where rye (Old English ryge) was grown, or perhaps an occupational name for someone who grew or sold it.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads so named, most of them from Old Norse rjóthr ‘clearing in a forest’.

variants.name: Ryer
meanings: English (Sussex and Kent): probably from Rye (see Rea ) + the suffix -er, and is thus synonymous with Middle English atte Rye, denoting someone who lived by a river or an island. This type of formation is common in Sussex and Kent.
Americanized form of German   Reier   ‘heron’ or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.
Americanized form of Slovenian Rajar or Rajer (see Rayer ).

variants.name: Romney
meanings: English: habitational name from Romney (Kent), from an uncertain first element (perhaps an Old English personal name Rūmen) + Old English ēa ‘river, stream’. Compare Rye 2.

variants.name: Rea
meanings: English: from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atter ye ‘at the island’ (Old English ēg, īeg). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on an island or patch of firm ground surrounded by marsh, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Rye (Sussex), The Rye in High Wycombe (Buckinghamshire), Rye Hill in Epping Upland (Essex), Rye Mill in Feering (Essex), and Rye House in Stanstead Abbots (Hertfordshire). Alternatively, the name may also arise from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atter eye ‘at the river’ (Old English ēa) and may also be topographic, for someone who lived near a river or stream, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Rea in Hempsted (Gloucestershire), Ray Lodge in Lingfield (Surrey), and Rye Farm in Cranleigh (Surrey). Due to the similarity of Middle English forms it is now impossible to distinguish between the two explanations. See also Nye .

nameMeanings.name: Eudes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Croatian and Slovenian Rudež: nickname derived from rud ‘red, reddish’, or, alternatively, from rud ‘curly’ (standard Slovenian rod; compare Rodes ). In Croatia, the surname is also found in the form Rudeš.

variants.name: Rodes
meanings: Catalan (Rodés): habitational name from any of several places called Rodés, mainly those in El Pallars and El Conflent districts, in northern Catalonia.
Catalan: variant of Roda , from Catalan rodes, plural of roda ‘wheel’.
English: variant of   Rhodes  . Compare   Rhode  .

historicEvents.title: Vikings Invade England
historicEvents.description: Viking raiders from Scandinavia invaded England in 980. Years later in 991, the English government paid them 10,000 in hopes for peace. The Viking raiders, however, continued to force more money from England.

historicEvents.title: Danes Attack London
historicEvents.description: Danes and Norwegians attacked London unsuccessfully in 994.

historicEvents.title: St. Brice's Day Massacre
historicEvents.description: King Aethelred ordered all Danish men in England to be killed. It is unknown how many Danes were killed due to this order.

historicEvents.title: England Divided
historicEvents.description: King Canute died in 1035 and England was divided between his three sons. His son Sweyn received Norway, Harthacanute received Denmark, and Harold Harefoot received North England.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

en.lifeSummary: Baron Eudes De Rye was born in Ryes, Calvados, Normandy, France as the son of Hubert De Ryes Steward Of Normandy and Albreda Preaux. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Rohese FitzRichard de Clare. He died in 1079, in his hometown, and was buried in St John's Abbey, Colchester, Essex, England.

fr.St John's Abbey, Colchester, Essex, Engl: St John's Abbey, Colchester, Essex, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Baron Eudes De Rye est né à Ryes, Calvados, Normandie, France, fils de Hubert De Ryes Steward Of Normandy et Albreda Preaux. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Rohese FitzRichard de Clare. Il est décédé en 1079, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à St John's Abbey, Colchester, Essex, Angleterre.

Er hatte mit Rohese FitzRichard de Clare mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1079 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in St John's Abbey, Colchester, Essex, England begraben.

ja.St John's Abbey, Colchester, Essex, Engl: イギリスエセックスコルチェスタースト ジョン'ス アビー

ja.Colchester, Essex, England: イギリスイギリスエセックスコルチェスター
ja.lifeSummary: Baron Eudes De RyeはフランスノーマンディカルヴァドスリスでHubert De Ryes Steward Of NormandyとAlbreda Preauxの息子として生まれた。彼はRohese FitzRichard de Clareとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1079年に故郷で死去し，イギリスエセックスコルチェスタースト ジョン'ス アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.St John's Abbey, Colchester, Essex, Engl: 영국 에세스 코체스터 스트 존'스 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Baron Eudes De Rye(은)는 프랑스 노먼디 캘바도스 리스에서 Hubert De Ryes Steward Of Normandy(와)과 Albreda Preaux의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Rohese FitzRichard de Clare(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1079년 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 에세스 코체스터 스트 존'스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Baron Eudes De Rye родился в местности риесь, калвадос, норманди, Франция. Его родителей звали Hubert De Ryes Steward Of Normandy и Albreda Preaux. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Rohese FitzRichard de Clare. Он умер в 1079 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности ст джон'с эбби, колчестир, эссекс, Англия.
